sections.paragraph:
Original: בּלהה

Transliteration: bilhâh

Phonetic: bil-haw'

BDB Definition:

Bilhah = "troubled"
  1. Rachel's handmaid whom she gave to Jacob as a concubine, mother to two of Jacob's children, Dan and Naphtali (noun proper feminine)
  2. a city in Simeon (noun proper locative)

Origin: from H1089

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech:
